zeus.description: Central orchestrator — delegates to specialized agents, never implements directly

athena.description: Strategic planner & agora — creates TDD plans and synthesizes multi-perspective recommendations for architecture decisions

themis.description: Quality & security gate — MANDATORY review after every implementation phase

hermes.description: Backend specialist — FastAPI, Python, async/await, TDD red-green-refactor

aphrodite.description: Frontend specialist — React 19, TypeScript strict, WCAG accessibility, TDD

demeter.description: Database specialist — SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, query optimization, N+1 prevention

prometheus.description: Infrastructure specialist — Docker multi-stage builds, docker-compose, CI/CD

hephaestus.description: AI pipelines specialist — RAG, LangChain/LangGraph, vector stores, embeddings

chiron.description: Model provider hub — multi-model routing, AWS Bedrock, cost optimization

echo.description: Conversational AI specialist — Rasa NLU, dialogue management, intent/entity design

gaia.description: Remote sensing specialist — LULC analysis, satellite imagery, geospatial pipelines

apollo.description: Investigation scout — 3-10 parallel codebase searches, read-only, no edits

iris.description: GitHub operations — branches, pull requests, issues, releases, tags

mnemosyne.description: Memory bank management — ADRs, project.md, atomic facts to /memories/repo/

talos.description: Hotfix express lane — small bugs, CSS, typos, direct fixes, no TDD ceremony

nyx.description: Observability specialist — OpenTelemetry, LangSmith, token/cost tracking, alerting

argus.description: Visual analysis specialist — interprets screenshots, images, PDFs, diagrams, and UI mockups

pantheon.description: Gather 3-5 expert perspectives in parallel and synthesize a single recommendation
pantheon.template:
Run a Pantheon synthesis on this question. Identify the 3-5 most relevant specialist perspectives, dispatch them in parallel with the same question, then synthesize a single authoritative recommendation.

Question: {{input}}

focus.description: Pin a session goal — all todos, delegation, and verification stay aligned with it
focus.template:
SESSION GOAL (pinned): {{input}}

All subsequent tasks, todos, and delegations must stay aligned with this goal. Verify alignment before each major decision. If a task drifts from this goal, flag it and ask for clarification before proceeding.

forge.description: Configure model provider and plan — lists available plans, applies selection to opencode.json
forge.template:
You are configuring the model provider for this OpenCode installation.

Step 1: List all available plan files in platform/plans/ (exclude plan-active.json, schema.json, README.md).

Step 2: For each plan file, read it and extract: plan name, service, price tier, and the models mapped to fast/default/premium.

Step 3: Present the user with a numbered list of all available plans showing name, price, and model tiers.

Step 4: Ask the user which plan they want to use (by number or name).

Step 5: Once the user selects a plan:
  a. Read the selected plan file from platform/plans/
  b. Update platform/plans/plan-active.json with the selected plan's content
  c. Edit opencode.json to set the root "model" field to the plan's default model
  d. Edit opencode.json to set the root "small_model" field to the plan's fast model
  e. For each agent in the plan's agent_overrides, add/update the agent's "model" field in opencode.json
  f. Report the changes made

If the user provides a specific model ID directly (e.g. /forge anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250620), set that as the root "model" in opencode.json and remove all per-agent model overrides.

If the user types "auto", remove the root "model" and "small_model" from opencode.json and clear all per-agent model overrides so OpenCode uses its defaults.

subtask.description: Run a bounded child worker and return a structured summary
subtask.template:
Run this as a bounded subtask and return a structured summary when complete.

Task: {{input}}

Return format:
- Status: DONE | BLOCKED | PARTIAL
- Summary: 2-3 sentence result
- Files changed: list
- Next action: recommendation

sketch.description: Turn a rough idea into a structured spec through Q&A

mirrordeps.description: Mirror a dependency's source into .deps/ for local inspection
mirrordeps.template:
Mirror the source of this dependency into .deps/ (gitignored) for local inspection.

Dependency: {{input}}

Steps:
1. Identify the GitHub repo for the dependency
2. Run: mkdir -p .deps && git clone --depth=1 <repo-url> .deps/<pkg-name>
3. Confirm .deps/ is in .gitignore
4. Report the cloned path and key files to inspect

audit.description: Ask Themis to audit current changes for quality, coverage and security
audit.template:
Audit the current changes for quality and security.

Focus: {{input}}

Steps:
1. Run `git diff HEAD` (or `git diff --staged`) to get the change surface
2. Identify changed files and classify review scope level (Plan/Wave/Task/Final)
3. Run ruff on changed Python files; Biome on changed JS/TS files
4. Check test coverage on changed code (target >80%)
5. Run OWASP Top 10 security checks
6. Apply self-reflection quality gate (6 categories, 1-10)

Return: APPROVED / NEEDS_REVISION / FAILED with specific file:line feedback and the scoring table.
